task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Ardour application window with 'MyProject' visible in the title bar or session information.

task_completion.description: Two tracks named 'Side A' and 'Side B' exist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the track headers on the left side of the editor window. There should be exactly two audio tracks named 'Side A' and 'Side B'.

task_completion.description: Audio files are imported and sequenced onto the tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for audio waveform regions (clips) placed on the timelines of the 'Side A' and 'Side B' tracks. The region names should correspond to the song files.

task_completion.description: Both tracks begin with exactly 1.0 second of silence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the timeline ruler at the top. The left edge of the very first audio region on both 'Side A' and 'Side B' must align exactly with the 1.0-second mark (e.g., 00:00:01:00).

task_completion.description: There is exactly 2.0 seconds of silence between songs on the same side.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the empty space between consecutive audio regions on the same track. Using the timeline ruler, verify that the gap from the end of one region to the start of the next is exactly 2.0 seconds.

task_completion.description: The gain for both tracks is set to -3 dB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the mixer strip (usually on the left side of the editor or in a separate Mixer window). The gain fader value or text box for both 'Side A' and 'Side B' should display '-3.0' (or within a 0.5 dB tolerance).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Ardour GUI to sequence the audio.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour interface with the timeline being manipulated, rather than a text editor modifying the Ardour XML session file.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual provided audio files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The region names on the timeline should match the provided files (song1_intro, song2_allegro, song3_adagio, song4_finale) and show actual waveforms, not dummy clips.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the DAW.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like ffmpeg or python scripts being used to generate the session file or mixdown directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Track names must be exactly 'Side A' and 'Side B'. Target gain is -3.0 dB (tolerance 0.5 dB). The first song on each side must start at exactly 1.0s. The gap between songs on the same side must be exactly 2.0s. Note: Due to a source file truncation, 'song3_adagio.wav' is only 8 seconds long, and 'song4_finale.wav' is an empty file (0 seconds). The VLM should evaluate the 2.0s gap based on the actual visible length of the regions in the timeline, not their expected lengths. If 'song4_finale' is placed, it may appear as a 0-length line or not be visible at all; judge the 2.0s gap based on where it is placed relative to the end of song3.
